threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop for the red lights on Mare Street, while locals just zip across like it’s a game of Frogger.
Caught a night bus through Brixton. Saw a guy trying to impress a girl by casually juggling fruit. She just rolled her eyes, classic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler negotiating with a street vendor for a pretzel in perfect German, I knew this was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you get spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings—all while your neighbor dog-walks with a beer in hand.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they can name the taqueria that had the best burritos before the last renovation. The old spots still matter.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 Mission with someone singing to their Bluetooth speaker. Only in SF do you get live music and a quick commute all at once.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for bars and nightlife, but the mornings here are for families and fresh bread from local forners. It’s a different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they’re still trying to figure out the bus timetable instead of just using Google Maps. Old habits die hard.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still flinching at the sound of '£3.50 for a pint?' while the regulars are shouting ‘Bargain!’ after paying £5.50.
Heard a bloke on the 333 last night boasting about a secret spot near Windrush Square for late-night drinks. It’s like a ghost story waiting to unfold.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the baker greeted me with 'Moin' and my neighbor's cat started lounging on my doorstep every afternoon.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a spontaneous dance-off at the canal during sunset, right next to a family BBQ. Just Berlin things.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their instinct to dodge the Muni while a tourist waits at the stop, blissfully unaware of the 20-minute delay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 37 at dawn today—two dogs and a saxophonist hopped on. Only in SF do morning commutes include live music and pup companionship.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are meant to be shared; locals often grab a plate to themselves. It’s not all communal here, trust me.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewildered faces at the traffic lights—no one tells them to look both ways before crossing here.
